mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strengthening consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and supports higher equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the rank IC of RSAFS trend signal on 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 or the hit rate for positive returns does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 8.5%, it indicates severe labor market slack, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 8.5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time (majority positive).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic expansion, which quickly translates into positive short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS increase month-over-month, the average 5-day forward SPX return is not positive (mean <= 0).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) regime where the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, ultra-low discount rates compress equity risk premia and stimulate risk-taking, generating positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly above the unconditional mean (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals a sudden contraction in consumer spending, prompting negative revisions to near-term earnings expectations and immediate equity sell-off within five trading days.
failure_condition: On days following a >2% MoM decline in RSAFS, the average 5-day SPX return is positive or not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strengthening manufacturing and overall economic activity, which supports corporate profitability and drives positive equity returns over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change on subsequent 60-day returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising housing starts (HOUST) and a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reflects a vibrant housing sector supported by accommodative monetary policy, jointly predicting positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When HOUST rises month-over-month and FEDFUNDS is below its median, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600 thousand units, it signals a deep slump in residential investment, a key demand component, leading to prolonged negative consequences for equities over a quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 600, the average 60-day SPX return is not significantly lower than when HOUST >= 600 (Welch's t-test p > 0.05).
